title: Start the System From the System API Screen
summary: Initiate OptiSweep system startup from the system HMI by navigating to the system API screen and pressing the SYSTEM STARTUP control.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the operator needs to initiate system startup from the system HMI using the documented SYSTEM STARTUP control on the system API screen.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to verify startup completion, because the source excerpt does not provide confirmation indicators or completion criteria after pressing SYSTEM STARTUP.
Do not use this runbook for startup recovery, troubleshooting, or fault handling, because the source excerpt does not provide failure states or recovery actions.
Do not infer additional startup verification steps or recovery actions from this excerpt alone.

responsibilities: Navigate to the system API screen

safety_notes: This source describes a normal HMI operating action.
The source excerpt does not provide additional safety controls, interlocks, or warnings for this action.

system_preconditions: System API screen is available on the system HMI

visual_references.description: System HMI screen artifact associated with section 5.1 Starting the System.
System API Controls figure showing the System Startup control on the API screen.

steps.title: Navigate to the system API screen
steps.purpose: Reach the documented HMI screen used for system startup.
steps.instruction: On the system HMI, navigate to the system API screen.
steps.expected_result: The system API screen is displayed on the system HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator is on the system API screen.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access or identify the system API screen from the available source guidance.

actions.label: Open system API screen
actions.exact_action: On the system HMI, navigate to the system API screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System HMI screen associated with section 5.1 Starting the System.
System API Controls screen reference showing the System section of the API screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system API screen cannot be located using the source-provided guidance.
The displayed screen does not match the documented system API/System API Controls context.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 5.1: STARTING THE SYSTEM 1. On the system HMI, navigate to the system API screen:
The state of the system can be found in the System section of the API screen. The system can also be started, shut down, and purged using this section of the screen.

steps.title: Locate the SYSTEM STARTUP control
steps.purpose: Identify the documented startup control on the system API screen before initiating startup.
steps.instruction: Locate the control labeled SYSTEM STARTUP on the system API screen.
steps.expected_result: The SYSTEM STARTUP control is visible and identifiable on the system API screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The SYSTEM STARTUP control is present in the System section of the API screen.
steps.failure_condition: The SYSTEM STARTUP control is not visible or cannot be identified from the source-provided screen references.

actions.label: Find SYSTEM STARTUP
actions.exact_action: Locate the control labeled SYSTEM STARTUP on the system API screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Startup-related system HMI screen associated with section 5.1.
The System Startup control in the System API Controls figure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The SYSTEM STARTUP control cannot be found on the system API screen.
The available screen does not show the documented System section controls.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 5.1: STARTING THE SYSTEM 1. On the system HMI, navigate to the system API screen: 2. Press SYSTEM STARTUP.
Listed controls include Get System State, System Startup, System Shutdown, System Close Out, System Purge, and Reset Metrics.

steps.title: Press SYSTEM STARTUP
steps.purpose: Initiate the documented system startup command from the system API screen.
steps.expected_result: The system startup command is initiated from the system API screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The SYSTEM STARTUP action has been issued from the system API screen.
steps.failure_condition: The source excerpt does not provide confirmation indicators, failure states, or post-action verification details after pressing SYSTEM STARTUP.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The System Startup control used to initiate startup.
Startup-related system HMI screen associated with section 5.1.

stop_or_escalate_if: No source-supported confirmation indicator is available to verify the result from this excerpt alone.
Any unexpected response occurs after pressing SYSTEM STARTUP, because the source excerpt does not provide recovery or escalation actions.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 5.1: STARTING THE SYSTEM 2. Press SYSTEM STARTUP.
The system can also be started, shut down, and purged using this section of the screen.

success_criteria: The operator reaches the system API screen on the system HMI.
The SYSTEM STARTUP control is identified on the system API screen.
The SYSTEM STARTUP control is pressed to initiate the startup command.
healthy_conditions: The system API screen is accessible on the system HMI.
The SYSTEM STARTUP control is visible in the System section of the API screen.
The startup command is initiated from the documented HMI control.
failure_conditions: The system API screen cannot be located or accessed using the source-provided guidance.
The SYSTEM STARTUP control cannot be identified on the system API screen.
The source excerpt does not provide confirmation indicators, failure states, or escalation instructions after pressing SYSTEM STARTUP.
escalation_guidance: If the system API screen or SYSTEM STARTUP control cannot be located, stop and seek SME or local support guidance because the source excerpt does not provide alternate navigation or recovery steps.
If an unexpected condition occurs after pressing SYSTEM STARTUP, stop and escalate for review because the source excerpt does not define failure handling.
Do not add unsupported verification or recovery actions beyond what is documented in this source packet.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System HMI screen artifact associated with starting the system.
System API Controls figure showing the System Startup control.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: 5.1: STARTING THE SYSTEM 1. On the system HMI, navigate to the system API screen: 2. Press SYSTEM STARTUP.
The state of the system can be found in the System section of the API screen. The system can also be started, shut down, and purged using this section of the screen.
